description: DevDrive cache migration, LFG brandmark menubar icon, dock badge support, app icon pipeline

userStories.title: Add LFG_CACHE_DIR resolution to state.sh
userStories.description: Add a shared cache directory variable to lib/state.sh that all modules source. If DevDrive is mounted at /Volumes/900DEVELOPER, use /Volumes/900DEVELOPER/.lfg-cache/ as cache dir. Otherwise fall back to $LFG_DIR (current behavior). Create the cache dir if it doesn't exist. Export LFG_CACHE_DIR for all modules.
acceptanceCriteria: LFG_CACHE_DIR exported from state.sh
If /Volumes/900DEVELOPER is mounted, LFG_CACHE_DIR=/Volumes/900DEVELOPER/.lfg-cache
If not mounted, LFG_CACHE_DIR=$LFG_DIR (backward compat)
Cache dir is mkdir -p created on source
bash -n lib/state.sh passes

userStories.title: Update all module scripts to use LFG_CACHE_DIR for HTML output
userStories.description: Change HTML_FILE assignments in scan.sh, clean.sh, btau.sh, devdrive.sh, stfu.sh, splash.sh, dashboard.sh, and chat.sh from $LFG_DIR/.lfg_*.html to $LFG_CACHE_DIR/.lfg_*.html. Each module already sources state.sh so the variable is available.
acceptanceCriteria: All 8 module scripts use $LFG_CACHE_DIR for HTML_FILE
bash -n passes for all 8 scripts
Modules still source state.sh before using the variable

userStories.title: Update viewer.swift navigation paths to resolve cache dir dynamically
userStories.description: In viewer.swift, the navigate action hardcodes lfgDir + '/.lfg_*.html'. Change this to check /Volumes/900DEVELOPER/.lfg-cache/ first, then fall back to lfgDir. Also update the LFG_NO_VIEWER background command to pass the correct output path. Update the home action's splash path similarly.
acceptanceCriteria: viewer.swift checks DevDrive cache path first, falls back to lfgDir
Navigation to all modules works when DevDrive is mounted
Navigation still works when DevDrive is NOT mounted (fallback)
swiftc compiles without errors

userStories.title: Update LFG agents to use DevDrive cache paths
userStories.description: Update the lfg-scan, lfg-clean, lfg-backup, lfg-devdrive, and lfg-monitor agent specifications in ~/.claude/agents/ to reference LFG_CACHE_DIR awareness. Update the CLAUDE.md key paths table to document the new cache location.
acceptanceCriteria: Agent descriptions mention DevDrive cache awareness
CLAUDE.md key paths table updated with cache dir
No hardcoded /Users/jeremiah paths in agent specs for cache

userStories.title: Update ui.js refreshView to handle DevDrive cache paths
userStories.description: The LFG.refreshView() function in ui.js uses window.location.pathname to extract the module name via regex. This regex must work regardless of whether the HTML is served from /Users/jeremiah/tools/@yj/lfg/ or /Volumes/900DEVELOPER/.lfg-cache/. The existing regex .lfg_(\w+).html already handles this since it only matches the filename, but verify and add a comment.
acceptanceCriteria: refreshView regex works with both cache locations
node -e passes on ui.js

userStories.title: End-to-end verification and cache location notification
userStories.description: Add a startup notification via LFG.notifications.add() in splash.sh indicating which cache location is active (DevDrive or local fallback). Verify all syntax checks pass across all modules.
acceptanceCriteria: Splash shows notification indicating cache location (DevDrive or local)
bash -n passes for all module scripts
swiftc compiles viewer.swift and menubar.swift without errors

userStories.title: Menubar custom LFG brandmark icon replacing SF Symbol
userStories.description: Replace the generic SF Symbol (externaldrive.fill) in menubar.swift with the LFG brandmark (shield + hard drive platter) from assets/brand/lfg-brandmark.svg. Render the SVG as a programmatic NSImage drawn via NSBezierPath at 18x18pt in template mode so it adapts to light/dark menu bar. The brandmark has: shield outline path, 3 concentric circles (outer r=14, middle r=8, spindle r=3), read/write arm diagonal, bracket ticks. Simplify to essential recognizable elements at 18pt: shield outline + outer platter circle + spindle dot.
acceptanceCriteria: menubar.swift draws LFG brandmark as NSImage via NSBezierPath (no external file dependency)
Icon renders at 18x18pt in template mode (adapts to light/dark menu bar automatically)
Shield outline + platter circle + spindle dot are visually recognizable at 18pt
SF Symbol externaldrive.fill code is removed
swiftc compiles menubar.swift without errors
Text label 'LFG ...' still displays to the right of the icon

userStories.title: Dock icon badge for unread notification count
userStories.description: Add NSDockTile badge support to viewer.swift. When JavaScript sends a 'badge' message via the webkit message handler with a count, update NSApp.dockTile.badgeLabel. The ui.js notifications system should post badge count updates whenever notifications._updateBadge() runs. Clear the badge (set to empty string) when count is 0 or when user opens the notification panel.
acceptanceCriteria: viewer.swift handles 'badge' action from JS bridge with count parameter
NSApp.dockTile.badgeLabel set to count string (or empty when 0)
ui.js notifications._updateBadge() posts badge count via window.webkit.messageHandlers.lfg
Badge clears when user opens notification panel (count becomes 0)
swiftc compiles viewer.swift without errors

userStories.title: Verify and update app icon pipeline from brandmark SVG
userStories.description: Verify the icon generation pipeline: scripts/gen-icns.sh should convert assets/brand/lfg-brandmark.svg to a proper .icns file with all required sizes (16x16 through 512x512 @2x). The icon should show the shield+platter brandmark in blue (#4a9eff) and white on a dark (#141418) rounded-rect background. Ensure the Makefile icons target correctly chains to gen-icns.sh. Both LFG.app and LFG Menubar.app should use the generated AppIcon.icns.
acceptanceCriteria: scripts/gen-icns.sh generates assets/brand/AppIcon.icns from lfg-brandmark.svg
Icon includes all standard macOS sizes (16, 32, 64, 128, 256, 512 at 1x and 2x)
Icon has dark background (#141418) with rounded corners, brandmark in white/blue
Makefile 'icons' target produces valid .icns file
Both Info.plist and InfoMenubar.plist reference AppIcon correctly
